title: Codonanthe calcarata
taxonomy: Codonanthe calcarata is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant belongs to the genus Codonanthe, which is part of the order Lamiales. Members of the Bignonaceae family are commonly known as clockvine or trumpet vine, and are characterized by their tubular flowers and twining stems. Codonanthe calcarata is a rare and unique species within this family.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues due to its purported adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
